record.field: Reactive Resume is a privacy-first, open-source resume builder that lets you create, manage, and share professional resumes without giving up control of your data.

Reactive Resume is a privacy-first, open-source resume builder that lets you create, manage, and share professional resumes without giving up control of your data.

It provides a real-time editor with dozens of templates, drag-and-drop section layouts, and one-click PDF export, all without requiring an account for basic use. Designed for individuals who value privacy, it ships with no tracking or analytics by default and can be fully self-hosted in minutes via Docker, giving you complete ownership of your information. Optional AI assistance helps refine your writing.

Key features
  • Real-time WYSIWYG editor with dozens of customizable templates
  • Drag-and-drop section reordering and flexible page layouts
  • Client-side PDF export and shareable public resume links with view tracking
  • Privacy by design: no tracking or analytics, full data export
  • Secure accounts with passkeys and two-factor authentication
  • Multi-language interface and optional AI writing assistance
Best for

Job seekers, freelancers, and privacy-conscious users who want to build and maintain polished resumes on their own infrastructure, as well as teams or career-services providers looking to offer a self-hosted resume platform.

services: postgres: image: postgres:16-alpine volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_DB: postgres POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U postgres -d postgres interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal minio: image: minio/minio:latest command: server /data volumes: - ./minio_data:/data environment: MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${MINIO_ROOT_USER} MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_minio' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal chrome: image: ghcr.io/browserless/chromium:v2.18.0 extra_hosts: - host.docker.internal:host-gateway environment: TIMEOUT: 10000 CONCURRENT: 10 TOKEN: ${CHROME_TOKEN} EXIT_ON_HEALTH_FAILURE: 'true' PRE_REQUEST_HEALTH_CHECK: 'true' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_chrome' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal app: image: amruthpillai/reactive-resume:latest depends_on: - postgres - minio - chrome environment: PORT: 3000 NODE_ENV: production PUBLIC_URL: https://{{ instance.full_domain }} STORAGE_URL: https://{{ instance.full_domain }}/default CHROME_TOKEN: ${CHROME_TOKEN} CHROME_URL: ws://chrome:3000 DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/postgres ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET: ${ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET} REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET: ${REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET} MAIL_FROM: {{ MAIL_FROM }} STORAGE_ENDPOINT: minio STORAGE_PORT: 9000 STORAGE_REGION: us-east-1 STORAGE_BUCKET: default STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY: ${MINIO_ROOT_USER} STORAGE_SECRET_KEY: ${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD} STORAGE_USE_SSL: 'false' STORAGE_SKIP_BUCKET_CHECK: 'false' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
